steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The main clause “lent Fairuz a timeless, cross-generational appeal” is independent. The phrase following the blank—”her the moniker”—lacks an explicit subject.

steps.title: Apply the rule for participles after commas
steps.content: After a comma following an independent clause, a participle (not a finite verb) introduces a modifying phrase. Earning her the moniker is a participle phrase expressing the result of the main action. “Earned” and “had earned” create a comma splice (two independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction); “earn” is a bare infinitive unsuitable here. That matches choice C — C is correct.
